clusters.title: Jet Collision at LaGuardia Airport Claims Lives of Two Pilots
clusters.summary: A tragic accident occurred at New York's LaGuardia Airport when a jet collided with a fire truck, resulting in the deaths of two pilots. The incident has led to the temporary shutdown of the airport as investigations are underway to determine the circumstances surrounding the collision. This event raises concerns about safety protocols at busy airports and the coordination between aircraft and emergency vehicles.

items.description: The two pilots killed in the collision between a passenger jet and a Port Authority fire truck at New York's LaGuardia Airport late Sunday night have been named as Antoine Forest and Mackenzie Gunther

clusters.title: Colombian Military Plane Crash Claims at Least 66 Lives
clusters.summary: A Colombian military transport plane crashed shortly after takeoff from Puerto Leguízamo, resulting in the deaths of at least 66 individuals, with dozens more injured. The aircraft was carrying 121 people, primarily soldiers, when the incident occurred in the southern Amazon region. This tragic event highlights ongoing concerns regarding military aviation safety in Colombia.

items.title: Plane Crash Kills 66 From Military and Police in Colombia
items.description: A military aircraft transporting 128 troops and crew members was in an accident as it took off from southern Colombia, military officials said.

items.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66, head of armed forces says
items.description: A Colombian military transport plane crashed near Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, shortly after takeoff, killing at least 66 people and injuring dozens.

items.title: At least 66 killed after Colombian military plane crashes in southern Amazon
items.description:
Transport plane carrying soldiers and crew crashed shortly after takeoff from Puerto Leguízamo, deep in Colombia’s southern Amazon region

A Colombian military transport plane with 121 people on board, mostly soldiers, crashed shortly after takeoff in the country’s south, killing at least 66 people, authorities said.

The defence minister, Pedro Sánchez, said the accident happened as the Lockheed Martin Hercules C-130 plane was taking off from Puerto Leguízamo, deep in Colombia’s southern Amazon region, on the border with Peru, as it transported troops from the armed forces.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump Suggests Iran Open to Deal Amid Denials of Talks
clusters.summary: President Trump indicated that Iran may be interested in negotiating to end ongoing conflicts, prompting a rally in stock markets and a drop in oil prices. However, Iranian officials have dismissed these claims as 'fake news,' denying any discussions with the U.S. regarding a potential deal. The contrasting statements highlight the complexities of U.S.-Iran relations and the challenges in achieving diplomatic progress.

clusters.title: Quadruple Amputee Cornhole Player Charged in Fatal Shooting
clusters.summary: Dayton Webber, a professional cornhole player and quadruple amputee, has been charged with fatally shooting a man while driving in Maryland. The incident occurred on Sunday night, resulting in the death of a front seat passenger. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the shooting, which has drawn significant attention due to Webber's unique background.

clusters.title: Explosion at Texas Oil Refinery Causes Fire and Shelter Order
clusters.summary: An explosion at an oil refinery near the Texas coast led to a significant fire that produced large plumes of smoke. Authorities issued a temporary shelter-in-place order for nearby residents, which has since been lifted as the fire was extinguished. Fortunately, no injuries were reported in the incident.

items.title: Texas residents feel homes shake miles away from oil refinery explosion
items.description:
Images and videos online showed large plume of smoke and flames billowing out from refinery, but no one was injured

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

An oil refinery fire near the Texas coast was put out on Tuesday and a temporary shelter-in-place order was lifted, hours after a large explosion at the complex shot plumes of smoke into the air, officials said.

No one was injured in Monday’s explosion at the Valero refinery in Port Arthur, about 90 miles (145km) east of Houston, said Charlotte M Moses. The Port Arthur mayor had urged residents in parts of the west side of the city to stay put.

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Rubio to Testify Against Former Roommate in Lobbying Trial
clusters.summary: Former U.S. Senator Marco Rubio is set to testify in the trial of David Rivera, his former roommate and congressman, who faces allegations of secretly lobbying for the Venezuelan government under Nicolás Maduro. The trial highlights concerns over foreign influence in U.S. politics and the implications of lobbying activities during the Trump administration. Rivera's case has drawn attention due to his connections within Miami's political landscape.

clusters.title: Iran Threatens to Close Strait of Hormuz Amid U.S. Tensions
clusters.summary: Iran's military has warned it will 'completely' close the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. follows through on threats made by former President Trump regarding Iranian power plants. This escalation reflects ongoing tensions between the two nations, with both sides exchanging stark warnings about potential military actions. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway for global oil shipments, making the situation significant for international trade and security.

clusters.title: Former FBI Director Robert Mueller Passes Away at 81
clusters.summary: Robert Mueller, the former FBI Director known for his investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 presidential election, has died at the age of 81. His tenure was marked by the high-profile inquiry into ties between the Trump campaign and Russia, which has had lasting implications for American politics. Reactions to his passing have varied, reflecting the polarized views surrounding his work and its impact.

clusters.title: Bill Cosby Ordered to Pay Nearly $60 Million for 1972 Assault
clusters.summary: A California jury has awarded nearly $60 million to a woman who accused Bill Cosby of sexually assaulting her in 1972. The verdict comes after a civil trial where the jury found in favor of the plaintiff, highlighting the ongoing legal repercussions for Cosby related to past allegations of sexual misconduct. This case underscores the broader societal discussions surrounding accountability for sexual violence.

clusters.title: Dennis Coyle Released from Taliban Detention in Afghanistan
clusters.summary: Dennis Walter Coyle, an American researcher, has been released after being detained by the Taliban in Kabul for over a year without charges. His release comes shortly after the U.S. government classified Afghanistan as a 'state sponsor of wrongful detention.' Coyle is now returning to the United States following his release.

These actions highlight the challenges facing the sector amid rising defaults and liquidity issues.

clusters.title: Trump Votes by Mail Despite Criticism of Mail-in Voting
clusters.summary: President Donald Trump cast a mail ballot for a special election in Florida, despite his ongoing criticism of mail-in voting as a form of 'cheating.' This action has drawn attention given his public stance against the voting method, which he claims leads to widespread fraud. The incident highlights the contrast between his personal voting choices and his rhetoric on election integrity.

items.title: Trump, Who Calls Mail-in Voting ‘Cheating,’ Just Voted by Mail
items.description: President Trump has long fixated on mail-in voting to bolster his baseless claims of widespread voter fraud. But he recently used the method in a Florida special election.

items.title: Trump casts a mail ballot again in Florida even as he calls the method 'cheating'
